author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter V. 20–23.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday, so far as the Lord vouchsafed to bestow, we discussed with what ability we could, and discerned according to our capacity, how the works of the Father and of the Son are inseparable; and how the Father doeth not some, the Son others, but that the Father doeth all things through the Son, as through His Word, of which it is written, “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us to-day look at the words that follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And of the same Lord let us pray for mercy, and hope that, if He deem it meet, we may understand what is true; but if we should not be able to do this, that we may not go into what is false.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is better not to know than to go astray; but to know is better than not to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, before all things, we ought to strive to know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Should we be able, to God be thanks; but should we not be able meanwhile to arrive at the truth, let us not go to falsehood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are bound to consider well what we are, and what we are treating of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are men bearing flesh, walking in this life; and though now begotten again of the seed of the Word of God, yet in Christ renewed in such manner that we are not yet wholly rid of Adam.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For truly our mortal and corruptible part that weighs down the soul shows itself to be, and manifestly is, of Adam; but what in us is spiritual, and raises up the soul, is of God’s gift and of His mercy, who has sent His only Son to partake our death with us, and to lead us to His own immortality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son we have for our Master, that we may not sin; and for our defender, if we have sinned and have confessed, and been converted; an intercessor for us, if we have desired any good of God; and the bestower of it with the Father, because Father and Son is one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He was speaking these things as man to men: God concealed, the man manifest, that He might make them gods that are manifest men; and the Son of God made Son of man, that He might make the sons of men sons of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what skill of His wisdom He doeth this, we perceive in His own words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as a little one He speaks to little ones, but Himself little in such wise that He is also great, and we little, but in Him great.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He speaks, in deed as one cherishing and nourishing children at the breast that grow by loving.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We, however, understood it not that the Father doeth something separately, which when the Son seeth, Himself also doeth something of the same kind, after seeing His Father’s work; but when He said, “The Son cannot of Himself do anything, but what He seeth the Father doing,” we understood it that the Son is wholly of the Father—that His whole substance and His whole power are of the Father that begat Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But just now, when He had said that He doeth in like manner these things which the Father doeth, that we may not understand it to mean that the Father doeth some, the Son others, but that the Son with like power doeth the very same which the Father doeth, whilst the Father doeth through the Son, He went on, and said what we have heard read to-day: “For the Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things that Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again mortal thought is disturbed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father showeth to the Son what things Himself doeth; therefore, saith some one, the Father doeth separately, that the Son may be able to see what He doeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, there occur to human thought, as it were, two artificers—as, for instance, a carpenter teaching his son his own art, and showing him whatever he doeth, that the son also may be able to do it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Showeth Him,” saith He, “all things that Himself doeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it therefore so, that whilst He doeth, the Son doeth not, that He may be able to see the Father do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet, certainly, “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence we see how the Father showeth the Son what He doeth, since the Father doeth nothing but what He doeth through the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What hath the Father made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He made the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hath He shown the world, when made, to the Son in such wise, that the Son also should make something like it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then let us see the world which the Son made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, both “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made,” and also “the world was made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the world was made by Him, and all things were made by Him, and the Father doeth nothing save by the Son, where doth the Father show to the Son what He doeth, if it be not in the Son Himself, through whom He doeth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what place can the work of the Father be shown to the Son, as though He were doing and sitting outside, and the Son attentively watching the Father’s hand how it maketh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where is that inseparable Trinity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where the Word, of which it is said that the same is “the power and the wisdom of God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where that which the Scripture saith of the same wisdom: “For it is the brightness of the eternal light?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where what was said of it again: “It powerfully reaches from the end even to the end, and ordereth all things sweetly”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever the Father doeth, He doeth through the Son: through His wisdom and his power He doeth; not from without doth He show to the Son what He may see, but in the Son Himself He showeth Him what He doeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What seeth the Father, or rather, what doth the Son see in the Father, that Himself also may do?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps I may be able to speak it, but show me the man who can comprehend it; or perhaps I may be able to think and not speak it; or perhaps I may not be able even to think it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that divinity excels us, as God excels men, as the immortal excels a mortal, as the eternal excels the temporal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May He inspire and endow us, and out of that fountain of life deign to bedew and to drop somewhat on our thirst, that we may not be parched in this wilderness!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us say to Him, Lord, to whom we have learnt to say Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We make bold to say this, because Himself willed it; if only we so live that He may not say to us, “If I am a Father, where is mine honor? if I am Lord, where is my fear?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then say to Him, “Our Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom do we say, “Our Father”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the Father of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, then, who says “Our Father” to the Father of Christ, says to Christ, what else but “Our Brother”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, however, as He is the Father of Christ is He in like manner our Father; for Christ never so conjoined us as to make no distinction between Him and us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He is the Son equal to the Father, the eternal Son with the Father, and co-eternal with the Father; but we became sons through the Son, adopted through the Only-begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence was it never heard from the mouth of our Lord Jesus Christ, when speaking to His disciples, that He said of the supreme God His Father, “Our Father;” but He said either “My Father” or “Your Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He said not “Our Father;” so much so, that in a certain place He used these two expressions: “I go to my God,” saith He, “and to your God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did He not say, “Our God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Further, He said, “My Father, and your Father;” He said not, “Our Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He so joins as to distinguish, distinguishes so as not to disjoin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He wills us to be one in Him, but the Father and Himself one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How much soever then we may understand, and how much soever we may see, we shall not see as the Son seeth, even when we shall be made equal with the angels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we are something even when we do not see; but what are we when we do not see, other than persons not seeing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that we may see, we turn to Him whom we may see, and there is formed in us a seeing which was not before, although we were in being.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For a man is when not seeing; and the same, when he doth see, is called a man seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For him, then, to see is not the same thing as to be a man; for if it were, he would not be man when not seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since he is man when not seeing, and seeks to see what he sees not, he is one who seeks, and who turns to see; and when he has well turned and has seen, he becomes a man seeing, who was before a man not seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, to see is to him a thing that comes and goes; it comes to him when he turns to, and leaves him when he turns away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it thus with the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be it from us to think so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was never so that He was Son, not seeing, and afterwards was made to see; but to see the Father is to Him the same thing as to be Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we, by turning away to sin, lose enlightenment; and by turning to God we receive enlightenment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the light by which we are enlightened is one thing; we who are enlightened, another thing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the light itself, by which we are enlightened, neither turns away from itself, nor loses its lucidity, because as light it exists.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father, then, showeth a thing which He doeth to the Son, in such wise that the Son seeth all things in the Father, and is all things in the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by seeing He was begotten; and by being begotten He seeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not, however, that at any time He was not begotten, and afterwards was begotten; nor that at any time He saw not, and afterwards saw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in what consists His seeing, in the same consists His being, in the same His being begotten, in the same His continuing, in the same His unchanging, in the same His abiding without beginning and without end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not therefore take it in a carnal sense that the Father sitteth and doeth a work, and showeth it to the Son; and the Son seeth the work that the Father doeth, and doeth another work in another place, or out of other materials.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Son is the Word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father said nothing which He did not say in the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by speaking in the Son what He was about to do through the Son, He begat the Son through whom He made all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And greater works than these will He show Him, that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here again we are embarrassed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is there that may worthily investigate this so great a secret?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, in that He has deigned to speak to us, Himself opens it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He would not speak what He would not have us understand; and as He has deigned to speak, without doubt He has excited attention: for does He forsake any whom He has roused to give attentive hearing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have said that it is not in a temporal sense that the Son knoweth,—that the knowledge of the Son is not one thing, and the Son Himself another; nor one thing His seeing, Himself another; but that the seeing itself is the Son, and the knowledge as well as the wisdom of the Father is the Son; and that that wisdom and seeing is eternal and co-eternal with Him from whom it is; that it is not something that varies by time, nor something produced that was not in being, nor something that vanishes away which did exist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, that time does in this case, that He should say, “Greater works than these He will show Him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He will show,” that is, “He is about to show.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hath shown is a different thing from will show: hath shown, we say of an act past; will show, of an act future.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we do here, then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, He whom we had declared to be co-eternal with the Father, in whom nothing is varied by time, in whom is no moving through spaces either of moments or of places, of whom we had declared that He abides ever with the Father seeing, seeing the Father, and by seeing existing; He, I say, here again mentioning times to us, saith, “He will show Him greater works than these.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is He then about to show something to the Son, which the Son doth not as yet know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, do we make of it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we understand this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, our Lord Jesus Christ was above, is beneath.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When was He above?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He said, “What things soever the Father doeth, these same also the Son doeth in like manner.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence know we that He is now beneath?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence: “Greater works than these He will show Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Lord Jesus Christ, our Saviour, Word of God, by which all things were made, what is the Father about to show Thee, that as yet Thou knowest not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of the Father is hid from Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What in the Father is hid from Thee, from whom the Father is not hid?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What greater works is He about to show Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or greater than what works are they which He is to show Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when He said, “Greater than these,” we ought first to understand the works than which are they greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us again call to mind whence this discourse started.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was when that man who was thirty-eight years in infirmity was healed, and Jesus commanded him, now made whole, to take up his bed and to go to his house.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this cause, indeed, the Jews with whom He was speaking were enraged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He spoke in words, as to the meaning He was silent; hinted in some measure at the meaning to those who understood, and hid the matter from them that were wroth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this cause, I say, the Jews, being enraged because the Lord did this on the Sabbath, gave occasion to this discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore let us not hear these things in such wise as if we had forgotten what was said above, but let us look back to that impotent man languishing for thirty-eight years suddenly made whole, while the Jews marvelled and were wroth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They sought darkness from the Sabbath more than light from the miracle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Speaking then to these, while they are indignant, He saith, “Greater works than these will He show Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Greater than these:” than which?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What ye have seen, that a man, whose infirmity had lasted thirty-eight years, was made whole; greater than these the Father is about to show to the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What are greater works?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He goes on, saying, “For as the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Clearly these are greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Very much greater is it that a dead man should rise, than that a sick man should recover: these are greater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when is the Father about to show these to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the Son not know them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He who was speaking, did He not know how to raise the dead?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had He yet to learn how to raise the dead to life—He, I say, by whom all things were made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who caused that we should live, when we were not in being, had He yet to learn how we might be raised to life again?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, do His words mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He condescends to us, and He who a little before was speaking as God, now begins to speak as man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Notwithstanding, the same is man who is God, for God was made man; but was made what He was not, without losing what He was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man therefore was added to the God, that He might be man who was God, but not that He should now henceforth be man and not be God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then hear Him also as our brother whom we did hear as our Maker.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Maker, because the Word in the beginning; our Brother, because born of the Virgin Mary: Maker, before Abraham, before Adam, before earth, before heaven, before all things corporeal and spiritual; but Brother, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Judah, of the Israelitish virgin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If therefore we know Him who speaks to us as both God and man, let us understand the words of God and of man; for sometimes He speaks to us such things as are applicable to the majesty, sometimes such as are applicable to the humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the selfsame is high who was made low, that He might make us high who are low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, saith He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Father will show” to me “greater than these, that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To us, therefore, He is about to show, not to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And since it is to us that the Father is to show, for that reason He said, “that ye may marvel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has, in fact, explained what He meant in saying, “The Father will show” to me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did He not say, The Father will show to you; but, He will show to the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because also we are members of the Son; and like as what we the members learn, He Himself in a manner learns in His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How doth He learn in us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As He suffers in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence may we prove that He suffers in us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From that voice out of heaven, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not Himself that will sit as Judge in the end of the world, and, setting the just on the right, and the wicked on the left, will say, “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom; for I was hungry, and ye gave me to eat”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when they shall answer, “Lord, when saw we Thee hungry?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will say to them, “Since ye gave to one of the least of mine, ye gave to me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us at this time question Him, and let us say to Him, Lord, when wilt Thou be a learner, seeing Thou teachest all things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Immediately, indeed, He makes answer to us in our faith, When one of the least of mine doth learn, I learn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us rejoice, then, and give thanks that we are made not only Christians, but Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do ye understand, brethren, and apprehend the grace of God upon us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Marvel, be glad, we are made Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He is the head, we are the members: the whole man is He and we.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what the Apostle Paul saith: “That we be no longer babes, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But above he had said, “Until we all come together into the unity of faith, and to the knowledge of the Son of God, to the perfect man, to the measure of the age of the fullness of Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fullness of Christ, then, is head and members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Head and members, what is that?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ and the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We should indeed be arrogating this to ourselves proudly, if He did not Himself deign to promise it, who saith by the same apostle, “But ye are the body of Christ, and members.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whenever, then, the Father showeth to Christ’s members, He showeth to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A certain great but yet real miracle happens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a showing to Christ of what Christ knew, and it is shown to Christ through Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A marvelous and great thing it is, but the Scripture so saith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall we contradict the divine declarations?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Shall we not rather understand them, and of His own gift render thanks to Him who freely bestowed it on us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this that I said, “is shown to Christ through Christ”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is shown to the members through the head.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, look at this in thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose that with thine eyes shut thou wouldest take up something, thy hand knows not whither to go; and yet thy hand is at any rate thy member, for it is not separated from thy body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Open thine eyes, now the hand sees whither it may go; while the head showed, the member followed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, there could be found in thyself something such, that thy body showed to thy body, and that through thy body something was shown to thy body, then do not marvel that it is said there is shown to Christ through Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the head shows that the members may see, and the head teaches that the members may learn; nevertheless one man, head and members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He willed not to separate Himself, but deigned to attach Himself to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far was He from us, yea, very far.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What so far apart as the creature and the Creator?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What so far apart as God and man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What so far as justice and iniquity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What so far as eternity and mortality?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, so far from us was the Word in the beginning, God with God, by whom all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, was He made near, that He might be what we are, and we in Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt in (among) us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, He is about to show us; this He showed to His disciples, who saw Him in the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “As the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it that the Father some, the Son others?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely all things were made by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do we say, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ raised Lazarus; what dead man did the Father raise, that Christ might see how to raise Lazarus?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When Christ raised Lazarus, did not the Father raise him? or was it the doing of the Son alone, without the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Read ye the passage itself, and see that He invokes the Father that Lazarus may rise again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As a man, He calls on the Father; as God, He doeth with the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore also Lazarus, who rose again, was raised both by the Father and by the Son, in the gift and grace of the Holy Spirit; and that wonderful work the Trinity performed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not, therefore, understand this, “As the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will,” in such wise as to suppose that some are raised and quickened by the Father, others by the Son; but that the Son raiseth and quickeneth the very same whom the Father raiseth and quickeneth; because “all things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to show that He has, though given by the Father, equal power, therefore He saith, “So also the Son quickeneth whom He will,” that He might therein show His will; and lest any should say, “The Father raiseth the dead by the Son, but the Father as being powerful, and as having power, the Son as by another’s power, as a servant does something, as an angel,” He indicated His power when He saith, “So also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not so that the Father willeth other than the Son; but as the Father and the Son have one substance, so also one will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who are these dead whom the Father and the Son quicken?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are they the same of whom we have spoken—Lazarus, or that widow’s son, or the ruler of the synagogue’s daughter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we know that these were raised by Christ the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is some other thing that He means to signify to us,—namely, the resurrection of the dead, which we all look for; not that resurrection which certain have had, that the rest might believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Lazarus rose to die again; we shall rise again to live for ever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it the Father that effects such a resurrection, or the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay verily, the Father in the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently the Son, and the Father in the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence do we prove that He speaks of this resurrection?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When He had said, “As the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lest we should understand here that resurrection which He performs for a miracle, not for eternal life, He proceeded, saying, “For the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was speaking of the resurrection of the dead, that “as the Father raiseth the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will;” and immediately thereupon added as a reason, concerning the judgment, saying, “for the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why said He this, but to indicate that He had spoken of that resurrection of the dead which will take place in the judgment?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For,” saith He, “the Father judgeth no man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A little before we were thinking that the Father doeth something which the Son doeth not, when He said, “The Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things that Himself doeth;” as though the Father were doing, and the Son were seeing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this way there was creeping in upon our mind a carnal conception, as if the Father did what the Son did not; but that the Son was looking on while the Father showed what He was doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, as the Father was doing what the Son did not, just now we see the Son doing what the Father doeth not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How He turns us about, and keeps our mind busy!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He leads us hither and thither, will not allow us to remain in one place of the flesh, that by changing He may exercise us, by exercising He may cleanse us, by cleansing He may render us capable of receiving, and may fill us when made capable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What have these words to do with us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was He speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is He speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A little before, He said that the Father showeth to the Son whatever He doeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I did see, as it were, the Father doing, the Son waiting to see; presently again, I see the Son doing, the Father idle: “For the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, the Son is about to judge, will the Father be idle, and not judge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What am I to understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What dost Thou say, O Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art God the Word, I am a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost Thou say that “the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I read in another place that Thou sayest, “I judge not any man; there is one who seeketh and judgeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of whom sayest Thou, “There is one who seeketh and judgeth,” unless it be of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He maketh inquisition for thy wrongs, and judgeth for them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it to be understood here that “the Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us ask Peter; let us hear him speaking in his epistle: “Christ suffered for us,” saith he, “leaving us an example that we should follow His steps; who did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth; who, when He was reviled, reviled not again; when He suffered wrong, He threatened not, but committed Himself to Him that judgeth righteously.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is it true that “the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are here in perplexity, and being perplexed let us exert ourselves, that by exertion we may be purified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us endeavor as best we may, by His own gift, to penetrate the deep secrets of these words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It may be that we are acting rashly, in that we wish to discuss and to scrutinize the words of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet why were they spoken, but to be known?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did they sound forth, but to be heard?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why were they heard, but to be understood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let Him greatly strengthen us, then, and bestow somewhat on us so far as He may deem worthy; and if we do not yet penetrate to the fountain, let us drink of the brook.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, John himself has flowed forth to us like a brook, conveyed to us the word from on high.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He brought it low, and in a manner levelled it, that we may not dread the lofty One, but may draw nigh to Him that is low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By all means there is a sense, a true and strong sense, if somehow we can grasp it, in which “the Father judgeth not any man, but hath given all judgment to the Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is said because none will appear to men in the judgment but the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father will be hidden, the Son will be manifest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what will the Son be manifest?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the form in which He ascended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the form of God He was hidden with the Father; in the form of a servant, manifest to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not therefore “the Father judgeth any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son:” only the manifest judgment, in which manifest judgment the Son will judge, since the same will appear to them that are to be judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Scripture shows us more clearly that it is the Son that will appear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On the fortieth day after His resurrection He ascended into heaven, while His disciples were looking on; and they hear the angelic voice: “Men of Galilee,” saith it, “why stand ye gazing up into heaven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This same that is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come in like manner as ye have seen Him going into heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what manner did they see Him go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the flesh, which they touched, which they handled, the wounds even of which they proved by touching; in that body in which He went in and out with them for forty days, manifesting Himself to them in truth, not in falsity; not a phantom, or shadow, or ghost, but, as Himself said, not deceiving them, “Handle and see, for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That body is now indeed worthy of a heavenly habitation, not being subject to death, nor mutable by the lapse of ages.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not as it had grown to that age from infancy, so from the age of manhood declines to old age: He remains as He ascended, to come to those to whom He willed His word to be preached before He comes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus will He come in human form, and this form the wicked will see; both they on the right shall see it, and they that are separated to the left shall see it: as it is written, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they shall look on Him whom they pierced, they shall look on that same body which they struck through with the spear; for a spear does not pierce the Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This body, therefore, will the wicked be able to look on which they were able to wound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God hidden in the body they will not see: after the judgment He will be seen by those who will be on the right hand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, is what He means when He saith, “The Father judgeth not any man, but all judgment hath He given to the Son,”—that the Son will come to judgment manifest, apparent to men in human body; saying to those on the right, “Come, ye blessed of my Father, receive the kingdom;” and to those on the left, “Go into everlasting fire, which is prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, that form of man will be seen by the godly and by the wicked, by the just and the unjust, by the believers and unbelievers, by those that rejoice and by those that mourn, by them that trusted and by them that are confounded: lo, seen it will be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When that form shall have appeared in the judgment, and the judgment shall have been finished, where it is said that the Father judgeth not any, but hath given all judgment to the Son, for this reason, that the Son will appear in the judgment in that form which He took from us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall be after this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall be seen the form of God, which all the faithful are thirsting to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall be seen that Word which was in the beginning, God with God, by which all things were made?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall be seen that form of God, of which the apostle saith, “Being in the form of God, He thought it not robbery to be equal with God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For great is that form, in which, moreover, the quality of the Father and Son is recognized; ineffable, incomprehensible, most of all to little ones.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall this form be seen?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, on the right are the just, on the left are the unjust; all alike see the man, they see the Son of man, they see Him who was pierced, Him who was crucified they see: they see Him that was made low, Him who was born of the Virgin, the Lamb of the tribe of Judah they see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when will they see the Word, God with God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will be the very same even then, but the form of a servant will appear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The form of a servant will be shown to servants: the form of God will be reserved for sons.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore let the servants be made sons: let them who are on the right hand go into the eternal inheritance promised of old, which the martyrs, though not seeing, believed, for the promise of which they poured out their blood without hesitation; let them go thither and see there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When shall they go thither?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Lord Himself say: “So those shall go into everlasting burning, but the righteous into life eternal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, He has named eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has He told us that we shall there see and know the Father and Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What if we shall live for ever, yet not see that Father and Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, in another place, where He has named eternal life, and expressed what eternal life is: “Be not afraid; I do not deceive thee; not without cause have I promised to them that love me, saying, ‘He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me; and he that loveth me, shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will show myself to him.’”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us answer the Lord, and say, What great thing is this, O Lord our God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What great thing is it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wilt Thou show Thyself to us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, didst Thou not show Thyself to the Jews also?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did not they see Thee who crucified Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Thou wilt show Thyself in the judgment, when we shall stand at Thy right hand; will not also they who will stand on Thy left see Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it that Thou wilt show Thyself to us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do we, indeed, not see Thee now when Thou art speaking?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He makes answer: I will show myself in the form of God; just now you see the form of a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I will not deceive thee, O faithful man; believe that thou shall see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou lovest, and yet thou dost not see: shall not love itself lead thee to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Love, persevere in loving; I will not disappoint thy love, saith He, I who have purified thy heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why have I purified thy heart, but to the end that God may be seen by thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But this,” saith the servant, as if disputing with the Lord, “Thou didst not express, when Thou didst say, ‘The righteous shall go into life eternal;’ Thou didst not say, They shall go to see me in the form of God, and to see the Father, with whom I am equal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe what He said elsewhere: “This is life eternal, that they may know Thee the one true God, and Jesus Christ whom Thou hast sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And immediately, then, after the judgment mentioned, all which the Father, not judging any man, hath given to the Son, what shall be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews honor the Father, despise the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Son was seen as a servant, the Father was honored as God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Son will appear equal with the Father, that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This we have, therefore, now in faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not the Jew say, “I honor the Father; what have I to do with the Son?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him be answered, “He that honoreth not the Son, honoreth not the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou liest every way; thou blasphemest the Son, and dost wrong to the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Father sent the Son, and thou despisest Him whom the Father sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How canst thou honor the sender, who blasphemest the sent?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, says some one, the Son has been sent; and the Father is greater, because He sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Withdraw from the flesh; the old man suggests oldness in time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the ancient, the perpetual, the eternal, to thee the new, call off thy understanding from time to this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is the Son less because He is said to have been sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I hear of a sending, not a separation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet, saith he, among men we see that he who sends is greater than he who is sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it so; but human affairs deceive a man; divine things purge him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not regard things human, in which the sender appears greater, the sent less; notwithstanding, things human themselves bear testimony against thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as, for example, if a man wishes to ask a woman to wife, and, not being able to do this in person, sends a friend to ask for him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there are many cases in which the greater is chosen to be sent by the less.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, wouldst thou now raise a captious objection, because the one has sent, the other is sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sun sends out a ray, but does not separate it; the moon sends out her sheen, but does not separate it; a lamp sheds light, but does not separate it: I see there a sending forth, not a separation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou seekest examples from human things, O heretical vanity, although, as I have said, even human things in some instances refute thee, and convict of error; yet consider how different it is in the case of things human, from which you wish to deduce examples for things divine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A man that sends remains himself behind, while only the man that is sent goes forward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does the man who sends go with him whom he sends?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Father, who sent the Son, has not departed from the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the Lord Himself saying, “Behold, the hour is coming, when every one shall depart to his own, and ye will leave me alone; but I am not alone, because the Father is with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How has He, with whom He came, sent Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How has He, from whom He has not departed, sent Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In another place He said, “The Father abiding in me doeth the works.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, the Father is in Him, works in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father sending has not departed from the Son sent, because the sent and the sender are one.
